content_of_post_to_comment: Men who are not part of the solution are part of the problem. You can’t let comments slide, not reprimand bad behaviour in a workplace when you are a manager, laugh at sexist jokes, and be committed to fitting in with toxic men, and not harm women. Every time a man laughs at a sexist joke it adds another brick to the structure that keeps women small.
comment: The hardest moments are often the small ones because silence feels socially rewarded. Maybe the real test of culture isn't whether people reject sexism privately, but whether they risk a little status to interrupt it publicly.
